Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(snap): systemctl shim outputs state text for is-enabled/is-active

When a unit was not found in D-Bus, both is-enabled and is-active exited
silently with a non-zero code. execFileUtf8 then falls back to Node's
error message ('Command failed: /snap/.../systemctl --user is-enabled
openclaw-gateway.service') as the detail string, which does not match
any of the isSystemdUnitNotEnabled patterns, causing the caller to throw
instead of returning false.

Fix: emit the state word to stdout (matching real systemctl behaviour):
- is-enabled: 'not-found' + exit 4 when unit not in D-Bus
- is-active:  'inactive'  + exit 3 when unit not in D-Bus
- Both also echo the state when the unit IS found, mirroring systemctl

isSystemdUnitNotEnabled already includes 'not-found' in its pattern set,
so no TypeScript changes are needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: per-tenant rate limiting with admin CRUD (M-25) (#497)

Adds api/tenant_rate_limiter.py for per-tenant rate limit management
with in-memory cache (RLock-protected) and database persistence.
Extends api/database.py with TenantRateLimit model.
Extends api/limits.py with tenant-aware key function.
Adds admin endpoints for rate limit CRUD in api/routers/admin.py.

Fixes deadlock: _cache_lock changed from Lock to RLock since
_refresh_cache_if_needed holds the lock while calling
_load_tenant_rate_limits which also acquires it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Make settings feature modular with declarative registry

Create a modular settings system using a declarative registry pattern,
allowing settings to be defined once and rendered automatically in the
UI without code duplication.

Changes:
- Add settingsRegistry.ts with SETTINGS_CONFIG declarative definitions
  (SettingDefinition interface, validation helpers, 141 lines)
- Add SettingControl.tsx with reusable controls
  (slider, toggle, select, input types, 189 lines)
- Add settingsHooks.ts with useSetting() and useSettingGroup() hooks
  (individual and batch setting management, 150 lines)
- Refactor SettingsModal to use registry system
  (105 -> 55 lines, -50 lines, 47% reduction)
- Add comprehensive documentation to copilot-instructions.md
  (new Section 11: Settings Registry System with examples)

Benefits:
- New settings can be added by extending registry (no modal code changes)
- All controls reusable across the app
- Type-safe with full validation and defaults
- Individual controls independently testable
- Consistent with keyboard shortcuts architecture
- 480 lines of new reusable code
- 100% backward compatible

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: PDF upload with AI topic extraction

Implemented revolutionary PDF-to-lessons feature:

📚 Upload PDF Page (/upload-pdf)
   - Beautiful drag-and-drop interface
   - PDF file validation
   - Subject and grade selection (Matemática, Português, etc.)
   - Real-time processing feedback
   - Mobile-responsive design

🤖 AI Topic Extraction (Groq API)
   - API route: /api/extract-topics
   - Extracts chapter titles and topics from PDF text
   - Uses Groq LLaMA 3.3 70B model
   - Smart fallback to default topics if parsing fails
   - Returns structured JSON with topics array
   - Temperature 0.3 for consistent results

✨ Features:
   - Reads PDF as ArrayBuffer (simplified - production would use pdf.js)
   - Sends first 5000 chars to AI for analysis
   - AI identifies chapter structure and topics
   - Displays extracted topics in cards
   - Direct YouTube search links for each topic
   - "Buscar no YouTube" button per topic
   - Reset functionality to upload new PDF

🎨 UX:
   - Loading states with spinner
   - Success message with topic count
   - Error handling with friendly messages
   - Topic cards with chapter numbers
   - One-click YouTube search integration
   - Clean list/reset flow

🔗 Navigation:
   - Added to navbar: "📚 Upload PDF"
   - Accessible from main menu (desktop + mobile)

Default Topics (if AI fails):
   - Matemática: Frações, Decimais, Geometria, Álgebra, Equações
   - Português: Substantivos, Verbos, Análise Sintática, Literatura
   - História: Brasil Colônia, Império, República, Guerras
   - Geografia: Relevo, Clima, População, Economia, Cartografia
   - Inglês: Verb to Be, Present/Past Simple, Vocabulary, Reading

Technical:
   - TypeScript with proper types
   - Error boundaries for all async operations
   - ESLint compliant (no unused vars)
   - SSR-safe implementation

Future enhancements (optional):
   - Use pdf.js for better PDF parsing
   - YouTube Data API integration for auto-video search
   - Save extracted topics to user profile
   - Create custom playlists from extracted topics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: rectangle-drag plow/farm tools + fix PESTICIDE/MULCH routing bug

- PLOW, WATER, HARVEST, FERTILIZE, MULCH, PESTICIDE moved to RECT_FARM_TOOLS:
  drag shows yellow bounding box + tool icon + 'W×H = N tiles' count label
- Fence, road, REMOVE remain as paint-drag (freeform trail)
- Batch action now fires in handleMouseUp (not deferred to click event):
  browsers may not fire 'click' after a real drag, causing silent no-ops
- _batchJustApplied guard prevents double-apply when click also fires
- Fixed PESTICIDE/MULCH incorrectly listed in BUILDING_TOOLS — single-click
  was routing to handleBuildingClick (REMOVE path) instead of handleFarmClick
- Updated all 6 tooltips to mention rectangle drag
- Layer 8 canvas drag preview: DRAG_ICONS dict, paint vs rect branch,
  yellow bounding rect + count label for rect tools

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ubuclaw): skip loginctl linger check when running as snap

When running as a snap the gateway lifecycle is managed by snapd as a
user daemon (daemon-scope: user in snapcraft.yaml). loginctl is not
accessible from within the snap's confined environment, so
readSystemdUserLingerStatus() always returns null, producing the
misleading message 'Unable to read loginctl linger status. Ensure
systemd + loginctl are available.'

Snapd automatically manages lingering when the service is enabled via
`snap start --enable ubuclaw.service`, so the check is both unreachable
and unnecessary in this context.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add done-emission artifact gate in evolution executor (#66)

evolution-worker has no 'kind' field in box.config.json so workerKind=null,
which causes worker_runner.ts to skip validateWorkerContract. The executor
now calls checkPostMergeArtifact() directly before runVerificationCommands to
enforce that worker output contains a post-merge git SHA + raw npm test output.

If the gate fails the task is pushed back to 'rework' with a specific error
message identifying which artifact (SHA / test output) is missing.

commit.message:
feat: use evaluatePreDispatchGovernanceGate as single pre-dispatch decision source (#63)

Replace inline PAUSE_WORKERS guardrail check in runSingleCycle with a
call to evaluatePreDispatchGovernanceGate. This consolidates the pre-
dispatch decision surface and adds two previously missing checks to the
live dispatch path:

  - Dependency cycle detection (resolveDependencyGraph)
  - Governance canary breach detection with automatic rollback

The inline PAUSE_WORKERS block is removed; the gate handles it.
The per-plan freeze filter (evaluateFreezeGate) is preserved as a
subsequent plan-level pass after the global gate.

No imports changed — evaluatePreDispatchGovernanceGate is defined in
the same file and already used by integration tests.

commit.message:
ci: skip Node.js/UI build for wfctl and lsp-server binary builds (#378)

* Initial plan

* ci: skip Node.js and UI build for non-server binaries in release matrix

The build matrix in release.yml was running the Node.js setup and UI asset
build steps for all binary types (server, wfctl, lsp-server). The UI assets
are only required for the server binary since it uses Go embed to serve the
admin UI.

Add build_ui: true to the server matrix entry and guard the Node.js setup
and UI build steps with `if: matrix.build_ui`. The wfctl and lsp-server
entries omit the flag (evaluates to falsy) so those steps are skipped,
saving unnecessary work on each build.

The 10+ minute build times for server and wfctl are due to sequential
cross-compilation across 5 target platforms (linux/amd64, linux/arm64,
darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, windows/amd64), each taking ~3 minutes. This
is expected behavior; the Go build cache (823 MB) is restored but does not
include cross-compiled artifacts for non-native targets.

Co-authored-by: intel352 <77607+intel352@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GoCodeAlone/workflow/sessions/f3bf4297-d828-4692-8be5-71cc074a95ae

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: use REST API instead of GraphQL for PR creation

'gh pr create' uses the GraphQL createPullRequest mutation which fails
with 'Resource not accessible by integration' on fork repos. Switch to
'gh api repos/.../pulls' (REST) and 'gh api .../issues/.../labels'
(REST) which do not have this restriction.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: drop new upstream workflows added by merge, not just conflicting ones

Switch from 'git merge --no-edit' (auto-commits) to 'git merge --no-commit'
so we can clean up before committing in all cases.

After the merge (clean or conflict-resolved), iterate .github/workflows/*.yml
and git-rm anything not in our whitelist (snap-build.yml, upstream-tag-watch.yml,
upstream-sync.yml). This prevents new workflows added by upstream from silently
landing in the fork on every sync.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: fix upstream-sync fetch - also fetch branches to get tag commit objects

'git fetch upstream --tags' fetches tag refs but the associated commit
objects may not be downloaded if no branch ref pulls them in. Adding a
plain 'git fetch upstream' first ensures all commit objects are present,
making the subsequent 'git merge <tag>' reliably work.

Also add a pre-merge check that fails fast with a clear error if the
resolved tag can't be found after fetch, instead of falling through into
the conflict handler with a misleading 'nothing to commit' error.

fix(test): wire npm test through run_tests.ts for deterministic .ts file discovery (#61)

node --import tsx --test without explicit file paths does not auto-discover
.test.ts files on Windows — Node's built-in test runner only auto-finds
.js/.mjs/.cjs test files. scripts/run_tests.ts already exists and correctly
enumerates all test files recursively before passing them to the test runner.

Wires npm test to use this script, resolving the Windows glob harness blocker
identified in the unified evolution plan as the primary gate blocker.

commit.message:
ci: fix upstream-sync YAML - use printf to avoid unindented heredoc content

Heredoc content at column 1 inside a YAML literal block scalar (run: |)
breaks the YAML parser. Replaced heredocs with printf format strings so
all content stays within the properly-indented block.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(orchestrator): use planIndex to actually remove critical-violation plans (#60)

The contract quality gate was calling plans.indexOf((r as any).plan)
but validateAllPlans results have no .plan property — so indexOf always
returned -1 and no plan was ever spliced out.

Fix: collect r.planIndex values, sort descending, and splice in reverse
order so earlier indices are unaffected by later removals.

Also adds a test verifying that the planIndex-based filtering pattern
produces the correct filtered array.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Box
repository.description: First step of agi

commit.message:
2x2 grid layout: text+capsule top, Mac/Win keyboards bottom

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: fix upstream-sync conflict commit and label application

Two bugs fixed:
1. When src/ conflicts remain after auto-resolving our paths, the branch was
   pushed without a merge commit (HEAD was still at pre-merge). Fix: stage
   conflicted files with markers via 'git add' and commit them so the branch
   has real content for the PR diff.

2. 'gh pr create --label' was failing with 'label not found' even though the
   label exists. Fix: create the PR without --label, capture the PR number
   from the URL, then apply labels separately with 'gh pr edit --add-label'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Dual Mac/Win keyboards side-by-side, capsule below, Win key label, demo card titles in white cells

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: auto-resolve downstream-owned paths during upstream sync

When the upstream merge hits conflicts, automatically resolve files in
paths we always own (.github/, README.md, snap/, CHANGELOG.md):

- If the file exists in our HEAD: keep ours (handles content conflicts
  and upstream-modified-vs-our-modified cases).
- If we deleted it (modify/delete conflict): keep the deletion (e.g.
  workflows we removed stay removed after each upstream sync).

After auto-resolution, only flag as a conflict PR if unresolved
files remain (i.e. source file conflicts that need human review).
Otherwise commit the merge and open a clean auto-merge PR.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
